aboutSite.title: About the Site
aboutSite.missionText: To archive and protect Naji Al-Ali's visual legacy, and present it to new generations in an organized and accessible way, while preserving its political and cultural context.
aboutSite.whyText: Naji Al-Ali is one of the most prominent Arab artists who used cartoons as a tool of resistance and expression. His drawings represent an invaluable historical document that must be preserved and made available to the world.

aboutNaji.title: About Naji Al-Ali
aboutNaji.born: Born in 1937 in Al-Shajara village, Palestine
aboutNaji.bio: Naji Al-Ali is a Palestinian cartoonist considered one of the most prominent artists in Arab history. During his career, he drew more than forty thousand cartoons published in many Arab newspapers. He created the famous character Handala, a symbol of the Palestinian refugee child.

supporters.title: Supporters
supporters.thanks: Our gratitude to all who contributed to preserving this legacy.
